calendar.matters:
RECORD ON APPEAL Court Judgment-Small Claims Appeal
Off Calendar Jan-26-2006 Set For Trial On Jan-26-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 611

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-27-2006 Judgment Entered

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jan-26-2006 Continued To Jan-26-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jan-13-2006 Continued To Jan-26-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525
MINI MINUTES: 9:35am
Judge Pro Tem Jeffrey S. Ross, clerk-beth wilber, nothing on record:
Cause called for Small Claims appeals. Allan Segal, Esq. appears on behalf of appellant; Dennis J. Teal, respondent, appears in Pro Per. (Mr. Segal appears on behalf of the appellant, Mercury Insurance, Chao L. Shan appears as appellant in Pro Per, Dennis J. Teal appears as Pro Per respondent.)
Mr. Segal represents to the Court that he is appearing to have Mercury Insurance removed from the judgment, Mr. Chan appeared in response to the Notice of Appeal. (Mr. Chan does not speak English and is assisted by Cindy Wong in interpreting Cantonese to English, and English to Cantonese.) The Court, through, the interpreter questions Mr. Chan as to why he is present when Farmers Insurance filed the appeal. Mr. Chan tells the court that his understanding was that Farmers appealed on his behalf and that's why he was present.
Mr. Segal and Mr. Teal proceed to testify in the narrative. Evidence is reviewed but not retained. Allan Seqal, Esq. was sworn to testify re a conversation he had with a representative from Mercury Insurance.
10:25am
After discussion among Mr. Segal, and the two other parties in court, the court determines that it is in the best interests of Mr. Chan to continue the matter. Mr. Chan is asked to contact Farmers Insurance with the help of an interpreter and Mr. Segal is advised to try and settle the matter or resolve the confusion among insurance carriers and Mr. Chan.The Court dismisses Mercury Insurance from the action, and the small claims appeal is continued to January 26, 2006,, 9am, Dept. 525
